{{Quote|The ritual welcome of the sovereign king of Kashir, a world almost ready to join the Republic, is nothing to take lightly, Anakin. The results of our interaction could have far-reaching implications in the Republic's relationship with this planet.|Obi-Wan Kenobi, to Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="JedisWeapon" />}}
'''Kashir''' was an [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] situated in the [[Vardoss sector/Legends|Vardoss sector]]. It was home to the [[Kashirim]] cultural group, which acquired [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] infamy due to their socially-accepted practice of [[Thief/Legends|thievery]]. Shortly before the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] of [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Kashir sought admission to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and, to that end, the planet's [[King/Legends|king]], [[Sha-Mar Ma-dred]], visited the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where he had a ritual meeting with [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. Between [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] and [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]], Kashir was a member of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==Description==
Kashir was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="JedisWeapon" /> located in the [[Kashir system/Legends|Kashir system]], a part of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Vardoss sector/Legends|Vardoss sector]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==History==
{{Quote|And now my friends, feast with me. And we shall discuss further the coming together of my Kashir and your Republic|Sha-Mar Ma-dred, to Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="JedisWeapon" />}}
[[File:ShaMarMaDredAndHisWives-AJedisWeapon.jpg|thumb|right|300px|King Sha-Mar Ma-dred of Kashir (top left) and his wives traveled to Coruscant to meet with the Jedi (bottom, from left to right) Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker.]]
During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]],<ref name="CSWE" /> which began in [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Kashir became one of the few worlds to actively seek a membership in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="CSWE" /> At some point shortly before the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]]<ref name="CSWE Groshim">{{CSWECite|I|371|Groshim}}</ref> of [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> the planet's [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]-speaking [[King/Legends|king]],<ref name="JedisWeapon" /> [[Sha-Mar Ma-dred]],<ref name="CSWE Sha-Mar">{{CSWECite|II|274|Ma-dred, Sha-Mar}}</ref> accompanied by his four [[Marriage/Legends|wives]] and a pair of guards, traveled to the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], in order to meet with representatives of the Republic's [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and discuss Kashir's admittance to the Republic.<ref name="JedisWeapon" />

The ritual meeting with the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]—who characterized Kashir as being nearly ready to become a Republic member world<ref name="JedisWeapon" />—and his<ref name="CSWE" /> Human [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], took place at the [[Republic Diplomatic Quarters]], with Kenobi warning Skywalker beforehand of the potential future significance of the event. During the meeting, both Ma-dred and Kenobi expressed their belief that Kashir's Republic membership would bring prosperity to that world.<ref name="JedisWeapon" /> Between [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] and [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]], Kashir was situated within the borders of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Kashirim}}
{{Quote|Anakin, the Kashirim are ''notorious'' throughout the galaxy for one thing: ''stealing''. A competent thief is considered a great value to their society. They are judged socially by their ability to pilfer.|Obi-Wan Kenobi, to Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="JedisWeapon" />}}
Kashir was home to the cultural group known as the [[Kashirim]],<ref name="CSWE" /> who were also known as the Kashir and included Humans, and was governed by a [[Monarchy/Legends|monarchy]] led by a king.<ref name="JedisWeapon" /> By the time of the Separatist Crisis,<ref name="CSWE" /> the Kashirim had become infamous throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for their [[Thief/Legends|thievery]], which in their society was a valued practice. Jedi Knight Obi-Wan Kenobi once described the Kashirim as being primitive.<ref name="JedisWeapon" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:AJedisWeapon.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Kashir was introduced in the comic story "A Jedi's Weapon."]]
Kashir was first mentioned in "[[A Jedi's Weapon]]," a [[Star Wars comic books|comic story]] written by [[Henry Gilroy]] and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] under the [[Infinities]] label in the [[Free Comic Book Day 2002 Special]]<ref name="JedisWeapon" /> on [[May 4]], [[2002]].<ref name="FCBD2002">The [[Free Comic Book Day 2002 Special]] was published on [[Free Comic Book Day]] in [[2002]], which was held on [[May 4]] according to {{WebCite|url=https://www.freecomicbookday.com|text=Home Page - Free Comic Book Day|author=[[Wikipedia:Diamond Comic Distributors|Diamond Comic Distributors]]|archivedate=20020120140609}}.</ref> The story was subsequently republished in the [[Star Wars Tales 12|twelfth issue]] of Dark Horse Comics' ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' comic book series<ref name="Tales12">[[Star Wars Tales 12|''Star Wars Tales'' 12]]</ref> that was published on [[June 5]] of the same year.<ref name="DarkHorse">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/11-031/Star-Wars-Tales-12-Photo-Cover|text=Star Wars Tales 12}}</ref> Plausible stories included in the [[Star Wars Tales 1|first]] through [[Star Wars Tales 20|twentieth]] issue of ''Star Wars Tales'' were deemed [[Canon|S-canon]] in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity until referenced in a non-''Tales'' source, at which point the subject matter was elevated to C-canon status.<ref name="TalesCanonicity">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=752#14952461|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-06-04|time=3:05 AM}}</ref> For the events of "A Jedi's Weapon" the latter took place when they were mentioned in [[Ryder Windham]]'s [[2005]] reference book ''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]''.<ref name="UVG">''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Kashir [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square J-8.<ref name="TEA" />
